title: Q3 Quiz 1 Draft: Production, Documentation, Planning, Setup, and Blockout

questions.text: What is the main purpose of a production log during the Q3 castle project?
choices: To track what was built, captured, changed, and planned next.
questions.correctAnswer: To track what was built, captured, changed, and planned next.
questions.explanation: A production log records work and decisions so the documentary has accurate process evidence.

questions.text: Which evidence best supports a making-of documentary?
choices: A short screen recording showing a decision being made.
A title with no visual proof.
questions.correctAnswer: A short screen recording showing a decision being made.
questions.explanation: Process evidence shows how the project changed, not just the final state.

questions.text: Why should a castle environment have a focal point?
choices: It helps the viewer know what to notice first.
It turns every mesh into a material.
questions.correctAnswer: It helps the viewer know what to notice first.
questions.explanation: A focal point gives the environment visual priority and direction.

questions.text: Why is player scale important in a third-person castle project?
choices: It shows whether doors, paths, walls, and spaces feel believable from player view.
It deletes the need for terrain.
questions.correctAnswer: It shows whether doors, paths, walls, and spaces feel believable from player view.
questions.explanation: The player character helps test whether the environment feels readable and walkable.

questions.text: Identify the term: supporting footage that helps explain a process, place, detail, or action.
questions.explanation: B-roll supports the main story with useful detail or process footage.

questions.text: Identify the term: a dated record of what was built, captured, changed, and planned next.
questions.explanation: The production log protects the process story from being forgotten.

questions.text: A student has a strong final screenshot but no process clips from the day. What should they do next class?
questions.correctAnswer: They should record the work process early, capture before/after evidence, and update the production log while they build.
questions.explanation: The documentary needs daily process evidence, not only final screenshots.

questions.text: A castle looks impressive from editor view but too small when the player walks through it. What should the student check?
questions.correctAnswer: They should test from player view and adjust scale, doors, paths, walls, or camera framing based on the character size.
questions.explanation: Player-view testing reveals scale problems that editor view can hide.

questions.text: Put the daily Q3 evidence workflow in order: build, record, log, upload, submit.
questions.correctAnswer: 1. Build or revise the castle. 2. Record screen/camera evidence. 3. Update the production log. 4. Upload evidence to Drive or YouTube. 5. Submit the link in DCC Creative Studio.
questions.explanation: This sequence keeps production work and evidence connected.

questions.text: In 2-3 sentences, explain why Q3 requires daily recording while students build the castle.
questions.correctAnswer: A strong answer explains that daily recording captures the process, decisions, mistakes, revisions, and progress that a final screenshot cannot show. It should connect the evidence to the final making-of documentary.
questions.explanation: This checks whether students understand the documentary purpose of daily evidence.
